hero.title: Sovereign infrastructure
hero.lead: We believe running advanced, capable models on hardware you already have — in small, efficient facilities — is how European SMEs compete with the hyperscaler mindset dominating today's global technology ecosystem.

intent.title: Why Helexa Exists
intent.p1: Today's AI infrastructure is dominated by hyperscale datacentres either physically located in jurisdictions outside the EU or under the control of legal entities that are answerable to jurisdictions outside the EU — with agendas, practices and strategies that may not always meet Europe's high ethical and consumer protection standards. European businesses must increasingly process and protect data within the EU regulatory framework, something external inference stacks rarely prioritise.
intent.p2Intro: Helexa leverages the infrastructure Europe already has and the open-weight models we cannot afford to lose:
intent.bullet1: Advanced, open-weight models running efficiently on commodity, professional and datacentre-class accelerators.
intent.bullet2: Data residency and privacy by default. Inference lives on infrastructure within the consumer's jurisdiction.
intent.bullet3: Leveraging currently available accelerator capacity without sacrificing performance.
intent.bullet4: Independence from global hyperscalers with competing interests.
intent.closing: Not a global cloud that happens to have an EU datacentre. Multiple independent, sovereign EU operators.

whyNow.title: Why Now
whyNow.problemBullet1: Today's inference stacks assume hyperscale datacentres, large GPU clusters and closed-source LLMs.
whyNow.problemBullet2: European regulation — GDPR, the AI Act, sectoral rules — directs workloads on-premises and in-region.
whyNow.problemBullet3: Sending data to foreign clouds is a regulatory, privacy and cost liability.
whyNow.problemBullet4: Cloud GPU access is expensive, supply-constrained and governed elsewhere.
whyNow.problemBullet5: Capable commodity and workstation hardware already sits underutilised across Europe.
whyNow.opportunityIntro: Near-frontier open-weight models now run well on hardware Europe already owns.
whyNow.opportunityBullet1: Advanced open-weight models run on workstations and commodity GPUs without datacentre GPU clusters.
whyNow.opportunityBullet2: European businesses want AI they can run locally, with predictable costs and guaranteed future access.
whyNow.opportunityBullet3: Operators with capable hardware can serve inference close to consumers.
whyNow.opportunityBullet4: Regulation is turning data sovereignty from a preference into a requirement.
whyNow.opportunityBullet5: Efficient inference software is the missing layer and the antidote to more and larger datacentres.

operators.title: Run advanced models on your own hardware.
operators.body: Helexa serves near-frontier open-weight models on commodity and workstation-class GPUs, on-premises, in your own jurisdiction. No cluster, no hyperscaler, no offshore dependency. Data stays where you control it.

routing.title: Requests stay close to home.
routing.body: Helexa routes each request to infrastructure with capacity, preferring region affinity, so traffic and data stay where you expect. Deployments compose into a wider network as capacity grows.

value.title: Work is proven. Economics are transparent.
value.body: Usage is metered transparently and independent operators will be compensated for the intelligence they serve. No platform tax, no opaque billing, no lock-in.

distributed.title: Sovereign by Design
distributed.body: European hardware, European operators, European law. No single point of failure and no external jurisdiction that access depends on.

participation.title: Runs on Hardware You Have
participation.body: From a single workstation to a regional datacentre, capable compute can run Helexa. It reduces infrastructure requirements rather than demanding new clusters.

fairness.title: Privacy & Transparency
fairness.body: Privacy-native: chat history lives only in the browser, never on a server. Usage metering is transparent; there are no black boxes and no hidden fees.

evolving.title: Resilient by Distribution
evolving.body: Capacity spread across many independent deployments is harder to censor, throttle or switch off, and resilient to foreign-policy shifts and single-vendor failure.

roadAhead.title: Where Helexa Is Going
roadAhead.p1: Our long-term goal is to become the software layer powering a distributed network of independently operated European AI infrastructure.
roadAhead.p2: From enterprise deployments running advanced models on their own hardware, to renewable-powered regional datacentres serving their communities.
roadAhead.p3: A European inference layer owned and operated in Europe. Resilient to outages, foreign-policy shifts, monopolies and single-vendor failure.
roadAhead.p4: Infrastructure that strengthens European autonomy instead of deepening dependency on a handful of foreign providers.

card.title: The software layer for European AI infrastructure
card.body: Helexa is early, and deliberately so. A working inference stack today, growing toward a distributed network of independently operated European deployments.

joinMesh.title: Sovereign AI Infrastructure.
joinMesh.lead: Whether you run AI on your own hardware, operate capacity, or are building toward European AI sovereignty, there is a place for you here.
joinMesh.footer: No walled gardens. A software layer for sovereign European AI. From a single workstation to renewable-powered regional datacentres.
